propositions: The completion verifier cannot mark criteria met when the done-evidence lives outside the codebase (deploy, live E2E, console setting, drill), forcing unsigned --force-close.
Introduce exactly two criterion kinds — codebase-verifiable (default, verified against code) and operator-attested (admits a concrete ticket-system attestation as evidence).

Classification is deterministic from an inline author tag: only exact case-insensitive [operator-attested] marks a criterion operator-attested; untagged or malformed tags default to codebase-verifiable.

An operator-attested criterion is MET only if an attestation names at least one verifiable specific (reference id/URL, actor, measured outcome, timestamp) that substantively matches; vague completion claims are NOT MET.

Split the injection guard: ticket text may never command a verdict, but a factual attestation reporting a checkable outcome is admissible evidence judged for substance, and a codebase-verifiable criterion is never satisfied by a comment alone.

Add an optional per-finding remediation field to VerdictFinding scoped to the completion verdict, plus new eval dataset cases, a case-conditional grounding rubric, unit tests, and ADR 0043 documenting the model.
